chapter_context.summary: Ahijah the prophet foretells the death of Jeroboam's son and the utter destruction of Jeroboam's family; Rehoboam's reign in Judah is marked by moral decline and an invasion by Shishak of Egypt.

outline.text: Jeroboam's Wife Disguises Herself to Consult the Blind Ahijah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam's Wife Disguises Herself to Consult the Blind Ahijah (14:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Ahijah Prophesies the Ruin of Jeroboam's House and Exile of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahijah Prophesies the Ruin of Jeroboam's House and Exile of Israel (14:7-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Death of Jeroboam's Son Abijah; Jeroboam's Death
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Death of Jeroboam's Son Abijah; Jeroboam's Death (14:17-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Rehoboam's Evil Reign in Judah; Rise of Sodomites and High Places
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rehoboam's Evil Reign in Judah; Rise of Sodomites and High Places (14:21-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Shishak of Egypt Sacks Jerusalem; Carries Away Gold Shields
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Shishak of Egypt Sacks Jerusalem; Carries Away Gold Shields (14:25-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Death of Rehoboam; Abijam Succeeds Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Death of Rehoboam; Abijam Succeeds Him (14:29-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 14, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
